name: Tommy Dorsey
description: American big band leader and musician
abstract: Thomas Francis Dorsey Jr. (November 19, 1905 – November 26, 1956) was an American jazz trombonist, composer, conductor and bandleader of the big band era.

works.t: Tess's Torch Song (I Had A Man)

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: I get a call from Tommy Dorsey’s office, would I join the Tommy Dorsey band... I played one tune before the intermission... I said, “Mr. Dorsey, Charlie Parker—” “I don’t give a shit about that” he says, “nobody quits my band. You’re fired.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Jones
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Tommy Dorsey in the context of Sy Oliver and Jimmy Lunceford, but the interviewee (Sam Jones) does not respond with any personal connection or anecdote regarding Tommy Dorsey.

about_quotes.speaker: Paul Smith
about_quotes.evidence: Dorsey I was with in ’47 and ’48... Tommy just fired his pianist... would you like to play one night with the band there? ... the old man wants to know if you want to join the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Paul Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: John Fedchock
about_quotes.evidence: my uncle sent me a double album set of Tommy Dorsey’s big band. He said if you play trombone you should know who Tommy Dorsey is. So I listened to that
about_quotes.doc_title: John Fedchock interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 28, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Wormworth
about_quotes.evidence: the records ranged from Tommy Dorsey’s band with one or two records where Buddy Rich played solos, to I guess Tommy Dorsey’s band with Jo Stafford
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Wormworth interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Noah Evans
about_quotes.evidence: Tommy Dorsey gave one of his arrangements to a band in Philadelphia... Tommy said, 'oh I want that arrangement'. So they traded arrangements.
